flags.message: The provisioned snapshot is a bare bulk-import docket: no docket entries, no dates, no docket number, and no petition documents. The only case-specific signal is the caption and one opinion cluster. The prediction therefore rests on recognizing the caption as the historical case Bartemeyer v. Iowa, 85 U.S. 129 (1874), plus corpus base rates; a predictor without background knowledge of this case could only emit the raw base rate.

This is a 19th-century writ-of-error merits case; the disposition enum (granted/denied/...) has no affirmed/reversed labels, and corpus stats show ~78% of resolved SCOTUS events reconcile to 'other'. Choosing between 'other' and 'denied' is mostly a guess about the reconciler's label mapping rather than legal uncertainty. A documented mapping for historical merits dispositions would make these events crisper.
